[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer (offer.buy=true) with maxAssets>0 and offerPrice < WAD (e.g. tickToPrice(MAX_TICK-tickSpacing)), under the precondition that buyerAssets = mulDivDown(1, offerPrice, WAD) == 0 for units=1, call take() with units=1 in a loop, violating the invariant that consumed[offer.maker][offer.group] must increase by at least 1 per fill when maxAssets>0, causing scoped impact: offer overfilled indefinitely with consumed never reaching maxAssets? Proof idea: fuzz offerPrice in [PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP, WAD-1], assert consumed[maker][group] strictly increases after every take with units=1.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer with maxAssets>0 and offerPrice in [1, WAD-1], under the precondition that mulDivDown(units, offerPrice, WAD) < units (rounding loss), call take() with units chosen such that buyerAssets rounds down to zero, violating the invariant that total units filled must not exceed maxAssets/offerPrice, causing scoped impact: maker's credit accumulates beyond intended maxAssets cap at zero asset cost to the maker? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given two offers from the same maker sharing the same group where offer A uses maxAssets>0 (consumed tracks buyerAssets) and offer B uses maxUnits>0 (consumed tracks units), under the precondition that both offers are valid and the group consumed counter is shared, fill offer A to set consumed=X (in asset units), then fill offer B which checks consumed<=maxUnits (in unit units), violating the invariant that the consumed counter must represent a single denomination across all offers in a group, causing scoped impact: offer B overfilled or underfilled because the counter mixes asset-denominated and unit-denominated values? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer with maxAssets>0 in a market where the settlement fee is subsequently increased by the feeSetter between two partial fills, under the precondition that buyerPrice = offerPrice (for buy offers, buyerPrice = offerPrice), call take() twice with the same units, violating the invariant that the consumed amount per unit must be consistent across partial fills of the same offer, causing scoped impact: maker's offer is undercharged (fewer assets consumed per unit after fee increase) or overcharged (more assets consumed per unit after fee decrease) relative to the original offer terms? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take, setConsumed] Can an unprivileged authorized operator of the maker, given a partially filled offer with consumed=X, under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true, call setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) during the onBuy callback of a take() to cancel the group, then observe that the take() that triggered the callback still completes successfully (consumed was already updated before the callback), violating the invariant that a cancelled offer must not be fillable in the same transaction, causing scoped impact: offer fill proceeds even though the maker cancelled the group mid-execution? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with maxAssets>0 where consumed tracks sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD), under the precondition that sellerPrice is very small (e.g. PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP=1e12) and units is chosen such that mulDivUp(units, 1e12, 1e18) = 1 for a large range of units values, call take() with units up to WAD/PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP-1 while consuming only 1 asset per fill, violating the invariant that consumed must accurately reflect the economic value of each fill, causing scoped impact: maker's sell offer is filled for far more units than maxAssets implies at the given price? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer with maxAssets>0 and a sell offer with maxAssets>0 from the same maker sharing the same group, under the precondition that the buy offer tracks buyerAssets and the sell offer tracks sellerAssets (which differ by the settlement fee), fill the buy offer for X buyerAssets (consumed=X), then fill the sell offer which checks consumed<=maxAssets using the same counter, violating the invariant that the group budget must represent a single consistent economic quantity, causing scoped impact: the sell offer's effective cap is reduced by the settlement fee spread from the buy fills, allowing the maker's total exposure to exceed the intended group budget? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer with maxAssets>0 and offerPrice exactly equal to WAD (tick=MAX_TICK), under the precondition that buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, WAD, WAD) = units, call take() with units=maxAssets to exactly exhaust the offer, then call take() again with units=0 (no-op), violating the invariant that a fully consumed offer must not trigger any state changes or callbacks, causing scoped impact: maker's callback is invoked on a fully consumed offer, potentially allowing the maker to observe and react to post-fill state? [File: 'src/interfaces/IERC20.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer with maxAssets>0 in market M1 and a sell offer with maxAssets>0 in market M2, both from the same maker with the same group, under the precondition that the consumed counter is global across all markets for the same (maker, group) pair, fill the buy offer in M1 to set consumed=maxAssets, then attempt to fill the sell offer in M2 which checks consumed<=maxAssets and reverts, violating the invariant that group budget exhaustion in one market must not prevent fills in other markets unless the maker intended OCO behavior, causing scoped impact: maker's sell offer in M2 is griefed by a taker who fills the buy offer in M1?
